name: New England Boiled Dinner (Clay Pot)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the clay pot in cold water for 10 minutes.
2. Place the corned beef in the pot.
3. Add the water to cover the meat by about an inch.
4. Add the herbs (bay leaf, thyme, parsley), onions, carrots, and celery.
5. Cover the pot and place it in a preheated oven set to 450°F.
6. Bake for 1 hour.
7. Add the prepared vegetables: cabbage cut into wedges, potatoes cut into halves, additional carrots cut into 1-1/2 inch pieces, parsnips cut into 1-1/2 inch pieces, and turnip cut into cubes.
8. Cover the pot again and continue cooking for another hour.
9. Serve with horseradish or a horseradish dressing made from sour cream, prepared horseradish, and lemon juice.
From "Cooking in Clay" by Irena Chalmers, Potpourri Press, Greensboro, N.C., 1974.
